claim: PCF with alpha(n)=-k*n^2, beta(n)=(k+1)n+k has p_n=(n+1)!*k^(n+1) and q_n=(n+1)!*sum_{j=0}^n k^(n-j)/(j+1); proof verified algebraically and convergents match for k=2 to 13 digits at n=14
Convergence rate of our PCF for k=2 is geometric with ratio ~1/2 (empirically 0.47 at n=14, predicted 1/2 by N!/(2N+1)!! ~ 2^-N analysis); abstract claim O(2^{-N}/N^{7/2}) consistent

Euler CF for ln(1+z) at z=1 (DLMF 4.9.1) reaches machine precision by N=22, MUCH faster than our PCF (still 1e-6 error at N=14); our PCF convergents are NOT a contraction or equivalence transform of Euler's CF for ln(2)
